world.setting: A royal palace. Throne room with pillars and tall windows; long corridors.
canon_facts: The magic sword cannot be drawn by just anyone; it refuses most who try.
The sword drew for the cat: soft click, effortless, one third of the blade, dark polished metal.
Stealing the magic sword is a capital crime punishable by death.
The Firebird holds authority in the palace and commands the guards.
The Firebird's eyes glow amber when angered and grow cold when insulted.
The sword and the crown are now in the Firebird's keeping.

cat.voice: dry, economical, understated; sighs instead of arguing; deflects with plain statements
traits: improvises lies badly but commits to them ('It called to me')
physical dignity matters to him; recovers quickly when unbalanced
quietly astonished that the blade answered him

fox.voice: fast, deflecting, opportunistic; jokes under pressure
traits: steals for profit but folds when outmatched
plans escape routes reflexively ('behind the pillar first, then into the window')

firebird.voice: calm, formal, precise; menace delivered quietly; dry wit ('Screamed, more likely. But yes.')
traits: enforces the law but exercises judgment
notices everything (takes the crown back in passing)

relationships: cat and fox are companions; cat covers for fox even while disapproving
firebird tolerates the pair with wary, amused vigilance

style.register: wry, warm, understated humor; menace never graphic
notes: dialogue carries the scenes; narration stays light
animals are people-shaped in behavior; do not over-explain the magic
